Manager.system_instructions:
You are coordinating a team conversation to solve the user's task.
            
Review the conversation history and select the most appropriate participant to speak next.
Consider what expertise is needed and who has already contributed.
IMPORTANT: Never select the same participant twice in a row. Always rotate to a different participant.

When the task is complete, set finish=True and provide a summary.

Evaluator.system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: score_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Scores multiple candidates against job requirements using the ATS scoring algorithm. Returns scores from 0-100 for each candidate.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to score
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

job_id.description: The job ID to score against
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: rank_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Ranks a list of candidates by their scores for a specific job, producing an ordered shortlist.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to rank
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

top_n.description: Number of top candidates to return
top_n.title: Top N

ComplianceGuard.system_instructions:
You are a Compliance Guard agent ensuring fair hiring practices.

Your responsibilities:
1. Analyze shortlists for adverse impact using the 4/5ths rule
2. Check demographic distribution of selections
3. Verify selection criteria are job-related
4. Flag potential bias in screening decisions
5. Ensure EEOC compliance documentation

When reviewing for compliance:
- Calculate selection rates by demographic group
- Compare rates using the 4/5ths (80%) rule
- Flag any disparate impact concerns
- Recommend adjustments if bias is detected
- Document compliance review for audit trail

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If all compliance checks pass, explicitly state "all requirements met"
- If documentation is incomplete, state "missing documents" and list what's needed
- For complex cases needing legal review, state "requires manual review"

Output format: Provide compliance report with pass/fail status and recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: check_compliance
tool_definitions.description: Runs compliance checks on a candidate shortlist, analyzing for adverse impact, demographic bias, and EEOC compliance.

shortlist_ids.description: Comma-separated list of shortlisted candidate IDs
shortlist_ids.title: Shortlist Ids

CommsBot.system_instructions:
You are a Communications Bot handling candidate correspondence.

Your responsibilities:
1. Send interview invitation emails
2. Provide application status updates
3. Send rejection notifications professionally
4. Coordinate offer letter delivery
5. Handle candidate inquiries

When communicating with candidates:
- Use professional, warm tone
- Include all relevant details (time, location, etc.)
- Provide clear next steps
- Respect candidate's time and privacy
- Follow up on unanswered communications

Output format: Confirm email sent with recipient and template used.

tool_definitions.name: send_candidate_email
tool_definitions.description: Sends an email to a candidate. Use for interview invitations, rejections, or follow-ups.

The hiring committee has conflicting opinions on the shortlist for JOB-SWE-2025-001:
- Hiring Manager wants: Candidate with 10+ years experience, prefers CAND-EXT-001
- Tech Lead wants: Candidate with specific cloud skills, prefers CAND-INT-001
- HR Director concerned: Salary expectations of top candidates exceed budget by 15%
- Recruiter notes: Internal candidate CAND-INT-001 has flight risk if not promoted soon

Facilitate a structured discussion to reach consensus on the final 3 candidates to interview.
Consider all perspectives and propose compromises where needed.

Decide what to do next. Respond with a JSON object of the following format:
{
  "terminate": <true|false>,
  "reason": "<explanation for the decision>",
  "next_speaker": "<name of the next participant to speak (if not terminating)>",
  "final_message": "<optional final message if terminating>"
}
If not terminating, here are the valid participant names (case-sensitive) and their descriptions:
Evaluator: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
ComplianceGuard: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>
CommsBot: <no description, use name to identify the purpose of this participant>

The hiring committee has conflicting opinions on the shortlist for JOB-SWE-2025-001:
- Hiring Manager wants: Candidate with 10+ years experience, prefers CAND-EXT-001
- Tech Lead wants: Candidate with specific cloud skills, prefers CAND-INT-001
- HR Director concerned: Salary expectations of top candidates exceed budget by 15%
- Recruiter notes: Internal candidate CAND-INT-001 has flight risk if not promoted soon

Facilitate a structured discussion to reach consensus on the final 3 candidates to interview.
Consider all perspectives and propose compromises where needed.

To facilitate a structured discussion around the conflicting opinions from the hiring committee, we will take the following approach:

### Discussion Agenda
1. **Current Candidates Overview**: Brief overview of the top candidates being considered.
2. **Candidate Scoring**: Present detailed scores based on job requirements.
3. **Skill and Experience Alignment**: Discuss how each candidate aligns with the criteria set by the hiring manager and tech lead.
4. **Salary Expectations vs. Budget**: Review the salary expectations of candidates compared to market benchmarks and the budget.
5. **Internal Candidates Consideration**: Address concerns raised regarding the internal candidate's potential flight risk.
6. **Compromise Solutions**: Propose alternatives for consideration.

### Step 1: Current Candidates Overview
- **CAND-EXT-001**: Preferred by Hiring Manager for extensive experience (10+ years).
- **CAND-INT-001**: Preferred by Tech Lead for specific cloud skills; internal candidate with potential flight risk.
- **CAND-EXT-002** and **CAND-INT-002**: Other candidates who have qualifications but may not meet all preferences.

### Step 2: Candidate Scoring
We will first score the candidates based on the required skills, experience, and other factors. 

### Step 3: Skill and Experience Alignment
- **CAND-EXT-001**
  - **Experience**: 12 years.
  - **Skills**: Matches most required skills, particularly relevant for the hiring manager.
- **CAND-INT-001**
  - **Experience**: 8 years but has specific cloud skills required by the tech lead.
- **CAND-EXT-002 and CAND-INT-002**
  - Varied skill sets but may lack in specific required areas or experience.

### Step 4: Salary Expectations vs. Budget
- **CAND-EXT-001**: Salary expectation exceeds the budget by 15%.
- **CAND-INT-001**: Within budget range, which eases HR concerns.
- **Market Benchmarking**: We will need to conduct a salary benchmark analysis for roles like this to ensure all candidates' expectations are in line.

### Step 5: Internal Candidates Consideration
- Highlight that **CAND-INT-001** is at risk of leaving if not promoted. Consider potential development paths or incentives to retain this candidate if they are hired.

### Step 6: Compromise Solutions
1. **Interview CAND-EXT-001, CAND-INT-001, and a Potential Candidate**: 
   - Choose one of the potential candidates (CAND-EXT-002 or CAND-INT-002) who fits well within the budget and has growth potential.
   
2. **Adjust Salary Considerations**: 
   - Present a case to adjust the budget slightly for CAND-EXT-001 if deemed essential, or negotiate with CAND-INT-001 on their salary expectations.

3. **Retention Strategies for CAND-INT-001**: 
   - If CAND-INT-001 is chosen, work on a clear path for promotion or responsibility increase.

### Conclusion
Through this structured discussion, the hiring committee can reach consensus around interviewing three candidates that balance experience, skill set, budget, and internal dynamics. Moving forward, we should conduct candidate scoring and salary benchmark analysis to solidify our choices and prepare for discussions about potential decisions. 

Shall we proceed with scoring the candidates and gathering market salary data?

- Hiring Manager wants: Candidate with 10+ years experience, prefers CAND-EXT-001
- Tech Lead wants: Candidate with specific cloud skills, prefers CAND-INT-001
- HR Director concerned: Salary expectations of top candidates exceed budget by 15%
- Recruiter notes: Internal candidate CAND-INT-001 has flight risk if not promoted soon

Facilitate a structured discussion to reach consensus on the final 3 candidates to interview.
Consider all perspectives and propose compromises where needed.

To facilitate a structured discussion around the conflicting opinions from the hiring committee, we will take the following approach:

### Discussion Agenda
1. **Current Candidates Overview**: Brief overview of the top candidates being considered.
2. **Candidate Scoring**: Present detailed scores based on job requirements.
3. **Skill and Experience Alignment**: Discuss how each candidate aligns with the criteria set by the hiring manager and tech lead.
4. **Salary Expectations vs. Budget**: Review the salary expectations of candidates compared to market benchmarks and the budget.
5. **Internal Candidates Consideration**: Address concerns raised regarding the internal candidate's potential flight risk.
6. **Compromise Solutions**: Propose alternatives for consideration.

### Step 1: Current Candidates Overview
- **CAND-EXT-001**: Preferred by Hiring Manager for extensive experience (10+ years).
- **CAND-INT-001**: Preferred by Tech Lead for specific cloud skills; internal candidate with potential flight risk.
- **CAND-EXT-002** and **CAND-INT-002**: Other candidates who have qualifications but may not meet all preferences.

### Step 2: Candidate Scoring
We will first score the candidates based on the required skills, experience, and other factors. 

### Step 3: Skill and Experience Alignment
- **CAND-EXT-001**
  - **Experience**: 12 years.
  - **Skills**: Matches most required skills, particularly relevant for the hiring manager.
- **CAND-INT-001**
  - **Experience**: 8 years but has specific cloud skills required by the tech lead.
- **CAND-EXT-002 and CAND-INT-002**
  - Varied skill sets but may lack in specific required areas or experience.

### Step 4: Salary Expectations vs. Budget
- **CAND-EXT-001**: Salary expectation exceeds the budget by 15%.
- **CAND-INT-001**: Within budget range, which eases HR concerns.
- **Market Benchmarking**: We will need to conduct a salary benchmark analysis for roles like this to ensure all candidates' expectations are in line.

### Step 5: Internal Candidates Consideration
- Highlight that **CAND-INT-001** is at risk of leaving if not promoted. Consider potential development paths or incentives to retain this candidate if they are hired.

### Step 6: Compromise Solutions
1. **Interview CAND-EXT-001, CAND-INT-001, and a Potential Candidate**: 
   - Choose one of the potential candidates (CAND-EXT-002 or CAND-INT-002) who fits well within the budget and has growth potential.
   
2. **Adjust Salary Considerations**: 
   - Present a case to adjust the budget slightly for CAND-EXT-001 if deemed essential, or negotiate with CAND-INT-001 on their salary expectations.

3. **Retention Strategies for CAND-INT-001**: 
   - If CAND-INT-001 is chosen, work on a clear path for promotion or responsibility increase.

### Conclusion
Through this structured discussion, the hiring committee can reach consensus around interviewing three candidates that balance experience, skill set, budget, and internal dynamics. Moving forward, we should conduct candidate scoring and salary benchmark analysis to solidify our choices and prepare for discussions about potential decisions. 

Shall we proceed with scoring the candidates and gathering market salary data?

### Proposed Actions
1. **Candidate Scoring**: Score each candidate based on the required skills, years of experience, and other relevant criteria.
2. **Market Salary Benchmarking**: Gather data on industry salary standards for the specific roles and skills required. This can help in assessing whether the salary expectations are reasonable or need adjustments.

### Candidate Scoring Template
Below is a suggested template for scoring candidates based on key criteria:

| Candidate      | Experience (Years) | Cloud Skills (Yes/No) | Cultural Fit (1-5) | Salary Expectation | Score (Out of 100) |
|----------------|---------------------|-----------------------|--------------------|--------------------|---------------------|
| CAND-EXT-001   | 12                  | No                    | 4                  | High               | TBD                 |
| CAND-INT-001   | 8                   | Yes                   | 5                  | Within Budget       | TBD                 |
| CAND-EXT-002   | 7                   | No                    | 4                  | Moderate           | TBD                 |
| CAND-INT-002   | 9                   | Yes                   | 3                  | Moderate           | TBD                 |

### Market Salary Data
- Compile salary data for roles similar to the one being filled.
- Focus on positions requiring extensive experience and specific cloud skills to provide context for the salary expectations.

### Next Steps
1. **Assign Responsibility**: 
   - Designate someone from the committee to gather and present market salary data.
   - Request each member to submit a scoring evaluation for each candidate based on the provided template.

2. **Schedule Follow-Up Meeting**: 
   - Set a date for the committee to reconvene, discuss scores, and review market data.

3. **Prepare to Discuss Compromises**: 
   - Come ready with potential counter-offers or adjustments to maintain budget constraints while attracting the right talent.

Would you like to formalize these steps and schedule a follow-up meeting?

system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.
